## [06:05 - 09:08]

information and not decide, right? So you need a decision. You need to make that transformation. They just want information. It's important to understand those two opposing agendas. So I'll still go through introduction, information gathering, I'll transition, I'll ask great questions, I'll drop a case study, tell a story, really give us some insight on the what, not the how, open some loops, there's a lot of value I can provide, and some things I want to tell them on another call, and then I'll book that second call. Now, keep in mind, my normal process is a one-call process. But because I'm sensing that the prospect's just definitely not in the pocket, there's no way they're going to make a decision, even if I just nail the entire thing, I'm going to on the fly adjust to a two-call. Also, they'll be spending more time with me, the more time they spend with me, the more likely they're going to make a decision, as long as that time's well spent, of course. And as you may have heard in sales, if we're not leading the call, if we're not the clear leader in the call, we're not going to get a decision. Doesn't mean we could still make the sale, right? But they'll be buying in spite of us, not because of us.
